title: The Candidate

jokes.text: Never assume, dear, It makes an ass out of both of us.
jokes.setup: Twyla apologizes for assuming something about Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira delivers a pretentious variation of the classic saying
jokes.explanation: Classic wordplay but Moira's delivery makes it feel more sophisticated than the original saying

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, you know what they say: never assume, because it makes an ass out of you and... me, I suppose, in this instance. Though I prefer the French version: 'assumer' — it's far more elegant.

jokes.text: I'd sooner poke my eyes out with hat pins.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggests council might be good for Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's characteristically dramatic and specific rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - overly dramatic with oddly specific imagery that only she would use

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Absolutely not. I am not going to sit around a table with a bunch of small-town bureaucrats and pretend to care about zoning permits and septic tank regulations. I have a reputation to maintain.

jokes.text: What's the word I'm trying to think of? / Well, let's hope we don't find it, okay?
jokes.setup: Moira struggles to find the right word to insult the town
jokes.punchline: Johnny desperately tries to prevent her from saying something awful
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff with Johnny as the voice of reason trying to contain Moira's snobbery

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This town is so... so...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Mom, don't.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: ...quaint.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh thank God.

jokes.text: Are we ready to order, councilor?
jokes.setup: After Moira's resistance to running for council
jokes.punchline: Twyla innocently keeps pushing the council idea
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Twyla's persistence despite Moira's clear rejection

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: But Mrs. Rose, you'd be so good at it. You're so organized, and you have great ideas about things.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Plus, my tarot cards said that someone close to me would be in a position of power soon.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I just assumed it was you.

jokes.text: It's just temporary storage, David. / Look at your things. Look at your life!
jokes.setup: David discovers clothes stuffed under his bed
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic reaction treating it like a life crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - taking a minor inconvenience and making it existential. Great quotable line

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What is this? What is all of this?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, are those my clothes?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Your clothes are under my bed? Under MY bed, Alexis. Do you understand what you've done? Do you have any idea what this means?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That I needed storage?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It means that my entire life has been a lie. That everything I thought I knew about my personal space, about boundaries, about the sanctity of my own bedroom — it's all been compromised. I'm going to need to sit down.

jokes.text: Look at your things. Look at your life!
jokes.setup: David finding Alexis's mess everywhere
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic escalation from complaining about things to existential life judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect David melodrama - takes a small complaint and makes it cosmic. Great escalation structure

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, there are wet towels on the bedroom floor. Again. Do you know what happens to hardwood when it's exposed to moisture? It warps. It buckles. It essentially gives up on life.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And these shoes—these are Louboutins, are they not? Yes. They're worth more than our first house, and you've just... abandoned them here like some sort of sad, red-soled monument to your complete lack of regard for anything.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know what? I don't even care about the shoes anymore. I care about what this says about you as a person. About us as a family. About the trajectory of Western civilization.

jokes.text: I wasn't drinking water. / Okay, so maybe it was mine, what difference does it make?
jokes.setup: David asks why clothes are wet
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that the mystery liquid might not be water
jokes.explanation: Typical Rose family chaos - casual about potentially gross situations

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why are all your clothes wet?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh, I was at the spa and I may have accidentally fallen into the hot tub.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: May have?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, I definitely fell in. But David, I'm not entirely sure it was water.

jokes.text: She could be a serial killer.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Alexis not knowing Twyla well
jokes.punchline: David's extreme paranoid conclusion about sweet Twyla
jokes.explanation: David's paranoia about the most innocent person in town

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, so you don't know anything about Twyla? At all?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, like, we work together, but I don't really know her.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God, okay. She's a serial killer. Twyla's a serial killer.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? No, she's not.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, you just said you don't know anything about her! That's exactly what someone would say if their coworker was a serial killer!

jokes.text: That she serves you... at the cafe. / Because we're friends.
jokes.setup: Alexis claims daily tea with Twyla proves friendship
jokes.punchline: David points out the transactional nature
jokes.explanation: David deflating Alexis's delusion about her friendships

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, Twyla and I have tea together every single day. That's what best friends do.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, you pay her to work here.

jokes.text: Stop doing that with your face!
jokes.setup: David making judgmental expressions about sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Alexis's exasperated reaction to David's visible judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - calling out non-verbal judgment

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not judging.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, your face is literally doing a judgement.

jokes.text: I could say something pretty inappropriate right now. / Well, I'm hoping you don't. / No, but I could, that's a real set up.
jokes.setup: Johnny says Ray's seat is 'up for grabs'
jokes.punchline: Bob acknowledging the double entendre potential but not making the joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor where the joke is about not making the obvious joke

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Ray's seat is up for grabs.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I heard what you said.

jokes.text: If I didn't know any better, I might think you had a crush on me! / Well, that's... That's funny.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting they'd work together closely
jokes.punchline: Johnny's oblivious joke creating maximum awkwardness for Bob
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Johnny's complete obliviousness makes Bob's discomfort funnier

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We'll be working very closely together. In fact, I'd say we'll be practically joined at the hip.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Well, that's... that's the idea, yes.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Two men, one space, sharing everything. It'll be like we're married.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I... okay.

jokes.text: Okay, gotta go, Bob.
jokes.setup: Escalating awkwardness of the crush conversation
jokes.punchline: Johnny's abrupt exit after creating maximum discomfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect button on the awkward scene - Johnny fleeing his own mess

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I think that's... that's wonderful. I'm very happy for you.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: And I should probably get going. I have a very important... thing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Goodbye.

jokes.text: Since you did tell me to break up with him, and then you dated him...
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about Alexis and Mutt breaking up
jokes.punchline: Revelation of Twyla's role in the breakup and subsequent dating
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing the messy romantic dynamics and Alexis's casual approach to betrayal

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're the reason Mutt and I broke up?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, he came into the café one day and he was really upset about you moving to the city, and I just... I listened to him. And we talked. And then we started seeing each other.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Twyla! You can't just date someone right after they break up with me!

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I know, I'm sorry. But we're actually really happy together now.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

jokes.text: if you can't be by yourself, then you probably shouldn't be in a relationship.
jokes.setup: Alexis giving relationship advice about being alone
jokes.punchline: The irony of this advice coming from someone desperately trying to avoid being alone
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Alexis spouting wisdom while clearly unable to follow it herself

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: The irony of this advice coming from someone desperately trying to avoid being alone

jokes.text: Twy? Call me if you get off work early!
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to play it cool about being alone
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining herself by desperately calling after Twyla
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Alexis can't maintain her cool facade

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm totally fine being alone. I don't need anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Twyla! Wait! Twyla, come back!

jokes.text: I just had to reschedule my colonoscopy.
jokes.setup: Roland ending a phone call as Johnny arrives
jokes.punchline: Casual oversharing of medical information
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland oversharing - perfectly in character but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, my hemorrhoids have been acting up again, so I'll have to call you back.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, I didn't need to know that.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, come on, Johnny. We're friends. You can't keep these things bottled up inside.

jokes.text: It takes a brave man to face defeat yet again, when his ego is so battered and bruised.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks for nomination forms
jokes.punchline: Roland's backhanded 'encouragement'
jokes.explanation: Roland's typical passive-aggressive 'support' - mean but in character

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'd like to get some nomination forms.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, nomination forms? Well, that's great, Johnny. That's really great. You know, I think you've got a real shot at this thing.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, not at all. But I admire the confidence.

jokes.text: Well, that's good to hear, so you're used to coming in second, huh?
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions running the second largest video chain
jokes.punchline: Roland twisting it into an insult about always losing
jokes.explanation: Great comeback that turns Johnny's accomplishment into an insult

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: The second largest? Well, that just means you're the first to lose.

jokes.text: Well, everyone knows everyone here, because there's only one restaurant.
jokes.setup: Roland warns about loss of privacy in politics
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry observation about the town's size
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response highlighting the absurdity of Roland's concerns in such a small town

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: In a town this size, there is no privacy in politics. Or anywhere else, for that matter.

jokes.text: Well, your son is 30, and you never see him.
jokes.setup: Roland talking about taking his boy for ice cream
jokes.punchline: Johnny's cutting observation about Roland's parenting
jokes.explanation: Johnny finally fighting back with a surprisingly cutting personal attack

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, that's very sweet, Roland. Although, I have to say, taking your son for ice cream doesn't really qualify as parenting. That's just... bribery with dairy.

jokes.text: You signed under 'Date.' / Yeah, like that's going to make a difference.
jokes.setup: Roland proudly gives Johnny his first signature
jokes.punchline: Roland's dismissive response when caught making an error
jokes.explanation: Roland's incompetence followed by his dismissal of his own mistake

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Is that your signature?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's my signature.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's just a line.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, a line's a signature.

jokes.text: she is going to a bar to have her needs met by some lovely gentleman she's yet to meet, who is cute enough to sleep with, but not nearly mature or stable enough to marry.
jokes.setup: David asks about Alexis's plans to get laid
jokes.punchline: Alexis's overly specific and calculating description of her ideal hookup
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - strategic approach to casual relationships with specific criteria

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So what's your plan to get laid?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I'm thinking someone who's attractive, but not so attractive that they know they're attractive. Someone with a good job, but not so good that they don't have time for me. And ideally someone who's never seen me in a bad light, which means I basically have to find someone who's never seen me.

jokes.text: 'Cause everyone will think we're together. / Okay, I think you're giving yourself a lot of credit. / I was being generous.
jokes.setup: Alexis says people would think they're together
jokes.punchline: Sibling insults escalating
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling dynamic with escalating insults

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: People would think we're together.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, that's a fate worse than death.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh please, you'd be so lucky.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Lucky? I'd rather date a mannequin.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: At least a mannequin has better taste in clothes.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Says the woman who wore a swimsuit to a funeral.

jokes.text: I heard that one, it's only because you converse with women easily, and you dress so well.
jokes.setup: Johnny mentions a rumor about him
jokes.punchline: Moira thinks he means gay rumors, not council rumors
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Moira and Johnny talking about completely different rumors

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: There's been a rumor going around about me.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Johnny. It's 2020. Who cares if you're gay?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm not gay. It's about the town council.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that's much worse.

jokes.text: If this continues, I'll have to call a neurologist.
jokes.setup: Confusion about which rumor they're discussing
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic medical conclusion about Johnny's confusion
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira overreaction - jumping to medical concerns for simple confusion

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I think what's happened here is you've suffered a minor stroke.

jokes.text: Trying to council to focus on the simplest thing is like... wrangling monkeys!
jokes.setup: Moira describing the difficulty of working with council
jokes.punchline: Her characteristically colorful comparison
jokes.explanation: Moira's vivid imagery for describing local politics

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Working with the town council is like trying to apply winged eyeliner in a hurricane while someone's actively criticizing your technique.

jokes.text: No, I'm just taking some 'me time.'
jokes.setup: Stevie asks if Alexis is waiting for someone at the bar
jokes.punchline: Alexis claiming to want alone time while clearly seeking company
jokes.explanation: Alexis continuing to pretend she wants solitude while clearly seeking interaction

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Are you waiting for someone?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: No, I just wanted some alone time.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You're sitting at the bar.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Alone time where people can see me.

jokes.text: Like, I know you dated David, but everyone's allowed a mistake, am I right?
jokes.setup: Alexis commenting on Stevie being a strong independent woman
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting her brother as a dating mistake
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling cruelty disguised as supportive girl talk

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, Stevie is a really strong, independent woman. She doesn't need a man to validate her.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Unlike some people.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh, please. I dated you for like three weeks.

jokes.text: You sound like my conscience.
jokes.setup: Alexis's comment about David being a mistake
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan response agreeing David was a mistake

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, he was.

jokes.text: I would rather be here by myself than engage in meaningless conversation with people I don't care about.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Stevie preferring to be alone
jokes.punchline: Stevie explaining her antisocial preference while talking to Alexis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - brutally honest about not caring about people while having this very conversation

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm not antisocial, I'm just... selective about who I spend my time with.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, but like, you don't spend time with anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Exactly. I'm very selective.

jokes.text: I'm currently running unopposed. So that kinda takes away the excitement of winning, you know, but still, I think it's gonna be a real hoot.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn announces her council candidacy
jokes.punchline: Her admission that winning by default isn't exciting but she's still enthusiastic
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's sweet obliviousness about the lack of competition

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I know winning by default isn't exactly exciting, but I'm still very enthusiastic about it.

jokes.text: just because you've spent all your time trying to sell the town and get outta here, so.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Moira potentially running for council
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's blunt observation about Moira's relationship with the town
jokes.explanation: Jocelyn's surprising moment of clarity about Moira's motivations

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Moira, honey, I love you, but the town doesn't really know you. You've been here for what, five years? And most people still think you're a little... strange.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Strange? I'm eccentric. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Right, well, most of the town just calls it 'that woman who wears the big wigs.'

jokes.text: If Jocelyn can do it, anyone can.
jokes.setup: Discussion about encouraging more people to run
jokes.punchline: Moira's backhanded compliment/insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira passive-aggressive comment disguised as encouragement

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I think it's wonderful that you're encouraging more people to run. It's very... charitable of you. After all, not everyone can be naturally gifted at it. Some people just need the extra motivation, you know? Though I suppose if we're being honest, some of those people probably shouldn't be running in public. But that's very altruistic of you to give them false hope.

jokes.text: Um, like, easy six.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about outfit danger level on a scale
jokes.punchline: David's casual assessment of potential violence
jokes.explanation: David's matter-of-fact assessment of danger in their sketchy bar plans

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: On a scale of 1 to 10, how much danger is this outfit in?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'd say a solid 8.

jokes.text: I'm the one who should be at a sketchy bar meeting randoms. I'm the one who should go missing!
jokes.setup: David going to sketchy bar without her
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dramatic claim that dangerous situations are her specialty
jokes.explanation: Alexis treating potential danger as a personal brand - perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, you can't go to a sketchy bar without me. Dangerous situations are literally my specialty.

jokes.text: you can snap the duct tape by just raising your hands over your head, and then bringing them down really hard.
jokes.setup: Giving David safety advice for sketchy bar
jokes.punchline: Casual, specific kidnapping escape knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - casually dispensing kidnapping survival tips like it's common knowledge from her past

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: If you're ever in a sketchy bar and someone tries to kidnap you, you have to go for the eyes first. Then you scream really loud so people know you're being kidnapped and not just, like, having a bad time.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's... oddly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I've been kidnapped twice.

jokes.text: So the two of them on council together? / Is there a more frightening thought?
jokes.setup: Discussing Roland and Jocelyn both being on council
jokes.punchline: Treating local politics like apocalyptic scenario
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their horror at small-town politics, shared married couple moment

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, this is it. This is how democracy dies.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, they're both on the town council.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, and now we're living in a Mad Max film, but with more cardigan sweaters and a Costco membership.

jokes.text: They would have a virtual monopoly on this town. / Well, don't they already?
jokes.setup: Moira worrying about Roland and Jocelyn controlling council
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry observation that they already control everything
jokes.explanation: Good reality check about the existing power structure

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm worried that Roland and Jocelyn are going to control the entire town council.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: They already do. They control everything.

jokes.text: Jocelyn practically begged me to take the bait!
jokes.setup: Johnny asking who suggested Moira run
jokes.punchline: Moira's interpretation of Jocelyn's innocent comments as manipulation
jokes.explanation: Moira reframing the situation to make herself seem like the victim of clever manipulation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, Jocelyn suggested it. She said, 'You'd make a great town council member.' I mean, at first I thought she was just being nice, but then I realized she was clearly manipulating me into running.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, that's... that's not manipulation. That's a compliment.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, is it? Because to me it sounded like psychological warfare wrapped in a cashmere cardigan.

jokes.text: I thought it would be me, but then you talked me out of it! But that's okay!
jokes.setup: Johnny processing that Moira is running instead of him
jokes.punchline: His attempt to be supportive while clearly bitter
jokes.explanation: Johnny's passive-aggressive response to being outmaneuvered

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, that's... that's wonderful. I'm very happy for you. Very happy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I mean, I was going to run, but you know, your timing is impeccable as always.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm kidding. This is great. This is really great. You're going to be fantastic.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Probably better than I would have been anyway.

jokes.text: It's like everybody saw you coming, and left.
jokes.setup: The bar being unusually empty
jokes.punchline: Stevie's brutal assessment of David's effect on the crowd
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - deadpan insult delivered with perfect timing

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You know, it's weird. The bar's been completely dead all night. Then David walked in and... nothing changed.

jokes.text: Well, if you start walking now, you'll get there before breakfast.
jokes.setup: David mentions a dating match 79 miles away
jokes.punchline: Stevie's practical but discouraging travel advice
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie practicality cutting through David's dating app optimism

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: 79 miles? That's like, what, a six-hour drive? By the time you get there, you'll hate them.

jokes.text: Well, if you start walking now, you'll get there before breakfast.
jokes.setup: David's 79-mile-away match
jokes.punchline: Stevie's sarcastic travel advice
jokes.explanation: Solid callback response but fairly predictable sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh, 79 miles? That's basically next door. You should definitely make the drive. What's 79 miles? That's like, what, ten minutes?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not ten minutes, Stevie.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I know it's not ten minutes, David. I was being sarcastic. But seriously, you should go. Love is worth the drive. You could make it a road trip. Stop for snacks, listen to some music, really enjoy the journey.

jokes.text: we are the only people here under sixty.
jokes.setup: Surveying the bar scene
jokes.punchline: Highlighting the demographic mismatch for their dating goals
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about the bar clientele not matching their expectations

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This is a nightmare. There's no one here.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, it's a Tuesday night at a small-town bar. What were you expecting?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't know, someone age-appropriate? Someone who doesn't have a reverse mortgage?

jokes.text: I notice that man is no longer sleeping under the pool table.
jokes.setup: Finding something positive about the bar situation
jokes.punchline: The 'improvement' being that someone woke up
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about the low bar for improvement at this establishment

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, the good news is someone woke up.

jokes.text: Do you wanna break? / Um, from what?
jokes.setup: Stevie suggesting pool
jokes.punchline: David's complete unfamiliarity with pool terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - wealthy background showing through complete ignorance of working-class activities

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: We could shoot some pool.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not familiar with that game.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: It's billiards. You use a cue stick.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't take requests.

jokes.text: It's like a drive-thru, except everybody has a gun.
jokes.setup: Alexis describing checkpoints in Johannesburg
jokes.punchline: Her casual comparison to fast food with deadly weapons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Alexis moment - casual comparison of dangerous situations to mundane experiences, revealing her wild past

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: They have these checkpoints in Johannesburg, right? And they're like... they're basically just like a Wendy's drive-thru, except instead of a Frosty, they give you a gun to the head.

tmdb_overview: Johnny considers running for town council. David and Stevie look for love. Alexis copes with being single.
insight: Character-driven episode lands 48 jokes in 41 minutes, balancing awkward family moments with political campaign chaos.
